behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in segmentation logic itself (single parser boundary). REAL-INFRA: behavior text is about splitting command text, not executing shell/OS/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected because behavior text does not require module handoff beyond the segmenter; System rejected because no real subprocess/network/OS primitive is required.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the segmenter with a multiline shell input and assert it returns distinct segments per newline-separated statement (including assignment lines and command lines), rather than one combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: incident was caused by classification of a whole multiline block as `not_a_trigger`, which appears at the boundary between segmentation and trigger classification. Unit on classifier alone can miss this interaction. REAL-INFRA: no need to run real `gh`; command text path is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior says 'block ... is recognized' (text classification), not real subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test the hook’s parsing/classification pipeline end-to-end in-process: feed a multiline command string with variable assignments then `gh pr create`, assert final classification is PR-create trigger.

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is the negative twin of behavior 2 and depends on the same segmentation+classification handoff, where false positives can arise from cross-segment logic. REAL-INFRA: no real `gh` execution needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior is trigger discrimination from command text, not OS/subprocess semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test the same parser/classifier path with multiline assignments plus `gh issue create`; assert result is not PR-create (e.g., `not_a_trigger` or issue-trigger) and no PR trigger flag is set.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is parser/extractor + state persistence module interaction. A pure unit on extractor or writer alone can miss contract mismatch. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem persistence is enough and fits Integration definition. No need for real network/CLI. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text requires persisting identifier for local review-loop state, not real external API/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test trigger->ID reconstruction->state write using a temp directory: provide a detected PR-create event payload, assert persisted state contains the expected PR identifier in the required field.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is an inter-module contract/handoff (PostToolUse writer -> PreToolUse reader/enforcer) via filesystem state; testing either side alone can miss schema/format drift. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem handoff is required but still Integration (local FS allowed). LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI model dependency. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text asks for readable/enforceable state format, not real subprocess/OS edge behavior beyond normal local file I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test both hook components against the same temp state file: run PostToolUse path to write review-gate state after PR detection, then invoke PreToolUse enforcement reader and assert it recognizes and enforces the gate.
